_comment: Runtime configuration for ai-code-cognitive-stress. Edit these defaults to change behaviour without touching code.
_comment_work_window: The work window is inferred per-user from interaction timestamps by default (p10–p90 of your daytime message hours, requiring at least 5 distinct weekdays of data). To PIN a manual window instead, add a 'work_window' block with 'start' and 'end' (HH:MM, local time) and it will override inference. Example: {"start": "09:00", "end": "19:00"}.

codl._comment: Engagement weighting for CODL. A session counts at full weight (1.0) only while you're actively driving it — within foreground_grace_minutes of one of YOUR messages. The rest of the time it is alive but 'cooking' in the background and counts at background_weight. Research basis: a background locus is the 'out-of-the-loop' supervisory regime, where monitoring is effortful and stressful (Warm et al. 2008; Endsley & Kiris 1995; Parasuraman & Manzey 2010) — discounted but never free. The magnitude (0.25) is borrowed from the prospective-memory monitoring cost of ~15-20% of ongoing-task capacity (Smith 2003; Masicampo & Baumeister 2011), at the conservative top of that range.

closure._comment: Real closure + rework event ingestion (git log + reflog), per-repo AND per-author attributed. 'autodiscover' (default true) finds repos from the working directories the agent sessions recorded, walking each cwd up to its nearest .git root — bounded to cwds the sessions touched, never walking the disk. 'repos' is an explicit list of absolute git repo paths, always scanned and unioned with discovered roots. 'identities' scopes closures to the operator's OWN commits (emails/names); empty → auto-discovered from local git config (global + each scanned repo's user.email/user.name, covering multiple accounts) — set only to add identities no local config records. From git reflog: a 'push' (update-by-push on a remote-tracking ref) is the strongest closure and inherently self-scoped (a server-side bot merge never writes it). From git log: commits/merges authored by the operator are closures, netted per-repo against loops opened in that repo (deficit = 1 - closed/correlatable, correlatable = loops in a repo the operator touched that day). A day with no git-correlatable activity of the operator's own is OMITTED as data (deficit = None, not 0), and the composite renormalises over the remaining axes. amend/rebase/reset/cherry-pick are REWORK and feed the Interruption axis instead, not closure. Local git only, read-only, nothing leaves the machine. Set autodiscover false AND leave repos empty to omit the Closure Deficit entirely — the axis has meaning only on git repos. Research basis: an open loop keeps consuming working memory until closed (Masicampo & Baumeister 2011); closure removes attention residue and an unclosed switch leaves residue (Leroy 2009); closure is a recovery resource (Sonnentag & Fritz 2007); demands without recoverable resources drive burnout (Demerouti et al. 2001).

scoring._comment: Composite-scoring scales and weights. Each axis is mapped to [0,1] by dividing by its ceiling (codl_avg / codl_ceiling, interruption_rate / interruption_ceiling; closure_deficit is already 0..1), then blended with 'weights' (codl, interruption, closure; normalized by their sum). Defaults are the v1 literature / null-hypothesis values: codl_ceiling=5 (one above Cowan's ~4 capacity limit), interruption_ceiling=10/hour, equal weights. These can be replaced with values suggested by population calibration (run: aicogstress --calibrate <export files>) — review the calibration report first. Calibrated weights are an unsupervised heuristic, NOT validated against felt load.
